old_claim.note: Banking provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Genoese notaries drafted and preserved the contracts that formed the early marine-insurance market and its enforceable documentary record.

source_shape.source_locator: Section 2 states that the first known policy was drawn up in Genoa by notary Tommaso Casanova on 18 March 1343 and describes later Genoese insurance regulation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The study directly locates early marine-insurance policies in Genoese notarial drafting and documentary practice.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The current Banking node is anchored to a later institution, whereas notarial records identify the concrete legal-document capability used by the first policy market.

ontology_before: Maritime Insurance depended on the later-scoped Banking node as a broad financial prerequisite.
ontology_after: Maritime Insurance depends on medieval notarial records as the documented contract-drafting and preservation layer for early policies.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: maritime_insurance now depends on notarial_records and no longer directly depends on banking.
would_reject_if: A source shows that the first Genoese standalone policies could not exist without the specific later banking institution represented by banking.
The notarial_records node is rescoped away from medieval Genoese commercial contracts.
short_reason: Notarial drafting is directly documented in the first policy; the later Banking node is not.
